round2_improvement_notes.linguistic: Agent provided no output in either round; no improvement possible.
round2_improvement_notes.landscape: Agent acknowledged other agents' findings (LHT, curb profiles) and used them to reinforce its own landscape-based reasoning, though the conclusion remained the same.
round2_improvement_notes.botanics: Agent explicitly cited the Landscape and Meta agents' architectural and infrastructure observations to justify its shift from medium to high confidence in the UK.
round2_improvement_notes.regulatory: Agent incorporated the Meta agent's specific observations about curb and paving styles to strengthen its regulatory inference.
round2_improvement_notes.meta: Agent integrated the Regulatory agent's LHT observation and Landscape agent's hedge identification to support its architectural analysis.

role_leakage_notes.botanics: Minor leakage: Cited 'British-style bungalows' (architecture/meta) and 'curb infrastructure' (regulatory/meta) to support botanical confidence.
role_leakage_notes.regulatory: Minor leakage: Cited 'British-style manicured hedges' (landscape/botany) and 'curb and paving styles' (meta) to support regulatory confidence.
role_leakage_notes.meta: Minor leakage: Cited 'LHT' (regulatory) and 'manicured hedges' (landscape/botany) to support architectural confidence.

verdict.judge_synthesis_notes: The judge correctly identified the lack of linguistic evidence and effectively synthesized the strong consensus from the other four agents. The reasoning clearly linked the visual evidence (LHT, bungalows, curbs, hedges) to the UK, explicitly eliminating Ireland based on the convergence of these specific markers.
